company: University of Texas, MD Anderson Cancer Center
intended_use: The Radiation Planning Assistant (RPA) is used to plan radiotherapy treatments for patients with cancers of the head and neck, cervix, breast, and metastases to the brain. The RPA is used to plan exte
intended_use_full: The Radiation Planning Assistant (RPA) is used to plan radiotherapy treatments for patients with cancers of the head and neck, cervix, breast, and metastases to the brain. The RPA is used to plan external beam irradiation with photon beams using CT images. The RPA is used to create contours and treatment plans that the user imports into their own Treatment Planning System (TPS) for review, editing, and re-calculation of the dose.
target_condition: Malignant cancers of the head and neck, cervix, breast, and brain metastases
ground_truth: Original clinical contours and dose distributions from actual patient treatment at MD Anderson Cancer Center and participating external institutions; physician-rated clinically acceptable plans for training data
other_metrics: Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) for body contouring > 0.8 for 95% of CT scans; recall (25th percentile) > 0.7 for target structures; isocenter detection within 3mm in all orthogonal directions for 95% of cases; dosimetric plan comparison metrics with <5-7% difference in passing rates between RPA and clinical plans for normal structures and target structures across cervix VMAT, cervix 3D, chest wall, head and neck, and whole brain treatment sites
